shared_properties: The Echo reuses Umbra’s silhouette family, palette discipline, and hard-edge lighting so all shadow actors feel related.
Every frame keeps authored transparency cues compatible with Bevy sprite tinting rather than premultiplying the halo into the whole frame.
The summon footprint aligns to the same floor baseline and 32 px placement grid used by the scale puzzle.
differences: The Echo omits jump and run motion and instead focuses on a calm shimmer that communicates fixed weight placement.
The frame interior uses more Shadow Teal edging than the player body so the support role is readable without UI text.
A brighter ground ring indicates pick-up eligibility and the exact support contact zone.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the shadow survival loop, mirror and prism puzzle rules, scale targets, HUD placements, and Bevy runtime constraints for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art deco tower mood, silhouette contrast, gold trims, and hard-edge lighting style.

reference_images.label: umbra_character_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra body proportions, head silhouette, claw shapes, and echo-friendly shadow anatomy.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load umbra_echo_marker.png during Loading and build a 2x2 atlas that supports idle shimmer, placed marker, and pickup-ready states for up to two active echoes.
runtime_notes: Render the echo at 50% opacity with additive edge light so it reads as allied shadow mass instead of a second player body.
Snap placement to the same 32 px grid used by the scale rig to keep torque calculations visually trustworthy.
Preserve one fixed foot marker and one soft halo so the echo remains readable when stacked near other weight objects.

context.usage: Displayed when the player summons or repositions an Echo to satisfy balance targets on puzzle floors.

silhouette_notes: Echo proportions mirror Umbra but stay shorter and rounder so the summon reads as support instead of primary control.
A faint ground halo marks the exact contact point used for torque math.
The head and shoulder silhouette must remain visible even at 50% opacity.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 192x288 px for four 96x144 frames with no frame gutter.
The contact ring remains centered beneath the body in every frame so torque readouts do not appear visually offset.
At 50% opacity the Echo stays readable over both Void Black and Amber Dusk test backgrounds.
